FormatFieldRef(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n40\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n41\t//\n42\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"\", \"\")\n43\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n44\tfunc custom message prefix\n203\t//\n204\t// Returns a formatted error message with value comparison.\n205\t//\n206\t// Example usage:\n207\t//\n208\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"\")\n209\t//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n210\t//\n211\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n212\t//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\n213\tfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n214\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n215\t\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n216\t\n217\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n218\t\n219\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n220\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n221\t\t}\n222\t\n223\t\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n224\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n225\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n226\t\t} else {\n227\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n228\t\t}\n229\t\n230\t\treturn builder.String()\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\t// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n234\t// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n235\t//\n236\t// Parameters:\n237\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n238\t//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n239\t//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n240\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n241\t//\n242\t// Returns a formatted range error message.\n243\t//\n244\t// Example usage:\n245\t//\n246\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n247\t//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\n248\tfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n249\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n250\t\n251\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n252\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n253\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n254\t\t}\n255\t\n256\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n257\t\t\tactualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n258\t}\n259\t\n260\t// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n261\t// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n262\t//\n263\t// Parameters:\n264\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n265\t//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n266\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n267\t//\n268\t// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n269\t//\n270\t// Example usage:\n271\t//\n272\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n273\t//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\n274\tfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n275\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n276\t\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n277\t\n278\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n279\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n280\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n281\t\t}\n282\t\n283\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n284\t\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n285\t}\n286\t\n287\t// =============================================================================\n288\t// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n289\t// =============================================================================\n290\t\n291\t// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n292\t// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n293\t//\n294\t// Parameters:\n295\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n296\t//\n297\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n298\t//\n299\t// Example usage:\n300\t//\n301\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeRequired)\n302\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n303\t//\n304\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeFormat)\n305\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n306\tfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n307\t\tswitch errorType {\n308\t\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n309\t\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n310\t\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n311\t\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n312\t\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n313\t\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n314\t\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n315\t\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n316\t\tcase ErrTypeType:\n317\t\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n318\t\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n319\t\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n320\t\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n321\t\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n322\t\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n323\t\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n324\t\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n325\t\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n326\t\tdefault:\n327\t\t\treturn string(errorType)\n328\t\t}\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\t// =============================================================================\n470\t// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n471\t// =============================================================================\n472\t\n473\t// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n474\t// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n475\t// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n476\t//\n477\t// Parameters:\n478\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n479\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n480\t//\n481\t// Returns a formatted error FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n494\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n495\t\n496\t\t// Add severity if requested\n497\t\tif includeSeverity {\n498\t\t\t// {\n502\t\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n503\t\t\t} else {\n504\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n505\t\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n506\t\t\t}\n507\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n508\t\t}\n509\t\n510\t\t// Add error type and field\n511\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n512\t\n513\t\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n514\t\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n515\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n516\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n517\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n518\t\t\t}\n519\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n520\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n521\t\t\t}\n522\t\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n523\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n524\t\t\t}\n525\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n526\t\t}\n527\t\n528\t\t// Add location if present\n529\t\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n530\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n531\t\t}\n532\t\n533\t\treturn builder.String()\n534\t}\n535\t\n536\t// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n537\t// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\n538\t//\n539\t// Parameters:\n540\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n541\t//\n542\t// Returns a brief error message string.\n543\t//\n544\t// Example =============================================================================\n563\t// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n564\t// =============================================================================\n565\t\n566\t// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n567\t// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n568\t//\n569\t// Parameters:\n570\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n571\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n572\t//\n573\t// Returns a formatted error list string.\n574\t//\n575\t// Example usage:\n576\t//\n577\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n578\t//\t    ErrorType: [High] [required] email: Field is required\n590\t//\t// 2. errors\"\n594\t\t}\n595\t\n596\t\tvar lines []string\n597\t\tfor i, err := range errors {\n598\t\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n599\t\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n600\t\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n601\t\t}\n602\t\n603\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n604\t}\n605\t\n606\t// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n607\t// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n608\t//\n609\t// Parameters:\n610\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n611\t//\n612\t// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n613\t//\n614\t// Example usage:\n615\t//\n616\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n617\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n618\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n619\t//\t}, {\n620\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n621\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n622\t//\t}}\n623\t//\n624\t//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n625\t//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\n626\tfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n627\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n628\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n629\t\t}\n630\t\n631\t\t// Count errors by type\n632\t\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n633\t\tfor _, err := range errors {\n634\t\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n635\t\t}\n636\t\n637\t\t// Build summary parts\n638\t\tvar parts []string\n639\t\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n640\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n641\t\t}\n642\t\n643\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n644\t}\n645\t\n646\t// =============================================================================\n649\t\n650\t// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n651\t// without the parent display.\n734\t// This is useful for keeping error messages concise.\n735\t// Unlike TruncateValue in format_helpers.go, this takes a string and maxLength.\n736\t//\n737\t// Parameters:\n738\t//   - usage:\n744\t//\n745\t//\ttruncated := TruncateString(\"very long string here\", 10)\n746\t//\t// Returns: \"very long...\"\n747\tfunc TruncateString(value string, maxLength int) string {\n748\t\tif maxLength < 3 {\n749\t\t\tmaxLength = 3\n750\t\t}\n751\t\n752\t\tif len(value) <= maxLength {\n753\t\t\treturn value\n754\t\t}\n755\t\n756\t\treturn value[:maxLength-3] + \"...\"\n757\t}\n758\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"73678e80-5ae1-4608-8e94-0759e50ddc23","uuid":"14e3bc77-f949-4e64-b46d-cdaefa82e175","timestamp":"2026-07-14T16:05:10.163Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/validate/error_formatting.go","content":"package validate\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n/*\nError Formatting Helpers\n\nThis file provides helper functions for formatting validation errors consistently.\nThese helpers work with the ErrorType enum, ErrorCategory, and ErrorSeverity types\nto produce human-readable, well-formatted error messages.\n*/\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FIELD REFERENCE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldRef creates a standardized field reference string with optional parent.\n// This ensures field names are consistently formatted across error messages.\n// Supports nested field paths and array index formatting.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The name of the field (e.g., \"email\", \"user.address\", \"users.0.email\")\n//   - parent: Optional parent path (e.g., \"request\", \"response.body\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\nfunc FormatFieldRef(fieldName string, parent string) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tparent = strings.TrimSpace(parent)\n\n\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Normalize parent path even when field is empty\n\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n\t\t\tif normalizedParent != \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\t\treturn normalizedParent\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn parent\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Normalize field name to handle array indices (e.g., \"users.0.email\" → \"users[0].email\")\n\tnormalizedFieldName := FormatFieldPath(fieldName)\n\n\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n\tif normalizedFieldName == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn parent\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t// Normalize parent as well in case it contains array indices\n\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n\n\t\t// If parent normalizes to unknown field, just return the field name\n\t\tif normalizedParent == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedParent, normalizedFieldName)\n\t}\n\n\treturn normalizedFieldName\n}\n\n// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\n// This is useful for error messages that need to show exactly where an error occurred.\n// Unlike FormatFieldLocation in format_helpers.go, this supports parent paths.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - location: Additional location context (e.g., \"line 5\", \"position 123\")\n//   - parent: Optional parent path\n//\n// Returns a formatted location string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"line 5\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email at line 5\"\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\nfunc FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName string, location string, parent string) string {\n\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n\tlocation = strings.TrimSpace(location)\n\n\tif location != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s at %s\", ref, location)\n\t}\n\n\treturn ref\n}\n\n// FormatFieldListWith formats a list of field names into a readable string with conjunction.\n// Unlike FormatFieldList in format_helpers.go, this supports custom conjunctions.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fields: Slice of field names\n//   - conjunction: The conjunction to use (e.g., \"and\", \"or\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\"}, \"and\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\"}, \"and\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email, password, and name\"\nfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n\t\treturn fields[0]\n\t}\n\n\tif len(fields) == 2 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Oxford comma style\n\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n// This ensures error messages are consistently formatted.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The type of error (use ErrorType enum or string)\n//   - message: The core error message\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\nfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\terrorType = strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add error type in brackets\n\tif errorType != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add field name if present\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - expected: The expected value\n//   - actual: The actual value\n//   - customMessage: Optional custom message prefix\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with value comparison.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n\t}\n\n\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n\t} else {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n//\n// Returns a formatted range error message.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n\t\tactualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n//\n// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//\n// Returns a formatted error type label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeRequired)\n//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeFormat)\n//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\nfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n\tswitch errorType {\n\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeType:\n\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(errorType)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\n// This converts technical error type strings to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string\n//\n// Returns a formatted error type label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"required\")\n//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"format\")\n//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\nfunc FormatErrorTypeFrom(errorTypeStr string) string {\n\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n\treturn FormatErrorType(et)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CATEGORY FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatCategory creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorCategory.\n// This converts technical category names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n//\n// Returns a formatted category label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryHTTP)\n//\t// Returns: \"HTTP Protocol\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryValidation)\n//\t// Returns: \"Data Validation\"\nfunc FormatCategory(category ErrorCategory) string {\n\tswitch category {\n\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n\tcase CategoryContent:\n\t\treturn \"Content\"\n\tcase CategoryValidation:\n\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n\t\treturn \"Performance\"\n\tcase CategorySecurity:\n\t\treturn \"Security\"\n\tcase CategoryCustom:\n\t\treturn \"Custom\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(category)\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// SEVERITY FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatSeverity creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorSeverity.\n// This converts technical severity names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n//\n// Returns a formatted severity label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityCritical)\n//\t// Returns: \"CRITICAL\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityHigh)\n//\t// Returns: \"High\"\nfunc FormatSeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tswitch severity {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"CRITICAL\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"High\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"Medium\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"Low\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"Info\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(severity)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FormatSeverityWithIndicator adds visual indicators to severity labels.\n// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n//\n// Returns a severity label with visual indicator.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityCritical)\n//\t// Returns: \"[!] CRITICAL\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityHigh)\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High\"\nfunc FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n\tlabel := FormatSeverity(severity)\n\n\tif indicator != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", indicator, label)\n\t}\n\n\treturn label\n}\n\n// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\nfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tswitch severity {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"!\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"⚠\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"■\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"○\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"i\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"?\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true)\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add severity if requested\n\tif includeSeverity {\n\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add error type and field\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n\n\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Add location if present\n\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//\n// Returns a brief error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n//\t// Returns: \"email: required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorBrief(err ValidationError) string {\n\tif err.FieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", err.FieldName, err.ErrorType)\n\t}\n\n\treturn err.ErrorType\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorList(errors, true)\n//\t// Returns:\n//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required\n//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format\nfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\tfor i, err := range errors {\n\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n//\n// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\nfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Count errors by type\n\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n\t}\n\n\t// Build summary parts\n\tvar parts []string\n\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n// without the parent parameter.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - location: Additional location context\n//\n// Returns a formatted location string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocation(\"email\", \"line 5\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email at line 5\"\nfunc FormatFieldLocation(fieldName string, location string) string {\n\treturn FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName, location, \"\")\n}\n\n// FormatFieldList is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldListWith\n// with \"and\" as the default conjunction.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fields: Slice of field names\n//\n// Returns a formatted list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldList([]string{\"email\", \"password\"})\n//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\nfunc FormatFieldList(fields []string) string {\n\treturn FormatFieldListWith(fields, \"and\")\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorToString is an alias for FormatValidationErrorFull.\n// This provides a more explicit name for converting a ValidationError to a string.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\nfunc FormatValidationErrorToString(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n}\n\n// TruncateValue is an alias for TruncateString for compatibility.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to truncate\n//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n//\n// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\nfunc TruncateValue(value string, maxLength int) string {\n\treturn TruncateString(value, maxLength)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// QuoteValue wraps a value in quotes for display in error messages.\n// Empty values return \"(empty)\" instead of quotes.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to quote\n//\n// Returns a quoted value string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"'email'\"\n//\n//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(empty)\"\nfunc QuoteValue(value string) string {\n\tif value == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"(empty)\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", value)\n}\n\n// TruncateString truncates a string value to a maximum length for display.\n// This is useful for keeping error messages concise.\n// Unlike TruncateValue in format_helpers.go, this takes a string and maxLength.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to truncate\n//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n//\n// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\ttruncated := TruncateString(\"very long string here\", 10)\n//\t// Returns: \"very long...\"\nfunc TruncateString(value string, maxLength int) string {\n\tif maxLength < 3 {\n\t\tmaxLength = 3\n\t}\n\n\tif len(value) <= maxLength {\n\t\treturn value\n\t}\n\n\treturn value[:maxLength-3] + \"...\"\n}\n","numLines":758,"startLine":1,"totalLines":758}}}

inputs\n\t\t\tstringResult := FormatError(tt.errorTypeStr, tt.message, tt.fieldName)\n\t\t\tenumResult := FormatErrorWithType(tt.errorTypeEnum, tt.message, tt.fieldName)\n\n\t\t\t// They should produce identical results\n\t\t\tif stringResult != enumResult {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatError and FormatErrorWithType should produce identical results:\\n  FormatError(string): %q\\n  FormatErrorWithType(enum): %q\",\n\t\t\t\t\tstringResult, enumResult)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify the result contains the error type\n\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(stringResult, tt.errorTypeStr) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Result should contain error type %q, got: %q\", tt.errorTypeStr, stringResult)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify the result contains the message\n\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(stringResult, tt.message) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Result should contain message %q, got: %q\", tt.message, stringResult)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify the result contains the field name (if provided)\n\t\t\tif tt.fieldName != \"\" && !strings.Contains(stringResult, tt.fieldName) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Result should contain field name %q, got: %q\", tt.fieldName, stringResult)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestFormatErrorWithType_AllErrorTypesProduceValidOutput tests that all ErrorType\n// enum values produce valid, well-formed output.\nfunc TestFormatErrorWithType_AllErrorTypesProduceValidOutput(t *testing.T) {\n\tallErrorTypes := []ErrorType{\n\t\tErrTypeRequired,\n\t\tErrTypeFormat,\n\t\tErrTypeRange,\n\t\tErrTypeLength,\n\t\tErrTypeType,\n\t\tErrTypeValue,\n\t\tErrTypeDuplicate,\n\t\tErrTypeConflict,\n\t\tErrTypeUnknown,\n\t}\n\n\ttestMessages := []struct {\n\t\tmessage   string\n\t\tfieldName string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\"Test message\", \"field\"},\n\t\t{\"Another test\", \"another_field\"},\n\t\t{\"Validation failed\", \"data\"},\n\t\t{\"Error occurred\", \"item\"},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, errorType := range allErrorTypes {\n\t\tfor _, testMsg := range testMessages {\n\t\t\tt.Run(errorType.String()+\"_\"+testMsg.fieldName, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorWithType(errorType, testMsg.message, testMsg.fieldName)\n\n\t\t\t\t// Verify result is not empty\n\t\t\t\tif result == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v should produce non-empty output\", errorType)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Verify result contains error type\n\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, errorType.String()) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v output should contain error type string, got: %q\", errorType, result)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Verify result contains message\n\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, testMsg.message) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v output should contain message, got: %q\", errorType, result)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Verify result contains field name\n\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, testMsg.fieldName) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v output should contain field name, got: %q\", errorType, result)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Verify consistent structure: [error_type] field: message\n\t\t\t\texpectedPrefix := \"[\" + errorType.String() + \"]\"\n\t\t\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(result, expectedPrefix) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v output should start with %v, got: %q\", errorType, expectedPrefix, result)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestFormatError_BackwardCompatibilityWithExistingFormatting tests that string-based\n// FormatError maintains backward compatibility with existing error formatting behavior.\nfunc TestFormatError_BackwardCompatibilityWithExistingFormatting(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname work correctly with both FormatError and FormatErrorWithType.\nfunc TestFormatError_ComprehensiveErrorTypeCoverage(t *testing.T) {\n\terrorTypeMappings := []struct {\n\t\tstringType string\n\t\tenumType   ErrorType\n\t}{\n\t\t{\"required\", ErrTypeRequired},\n\t\t{\"format\", ErrTypeFormat},\n\t\t{\"range\", ErrTypeRange},\n\t\t{\"length\", ErrTypeLength},\n\t\t{\"type\", ErrTypeType},\n\t\t{\"value\", ErrTypeValue},\n\t\t{\"duplicate\", ErrTypeDuplicate},\n\t\t{\"conflict\", ErrTypeConflict},\n\t\t{\"unknown\", ErrTypeUnknown},\n\t}\n\n\ttestScenarios := []struct {\n\t\tname      string\n\t\tmessage   string\n\t\tfieldName string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\"basic\", \"Test error\", \"field\"},\n\t\t{\"no_field\", \"Test error\", \"\"},\n\t\t{\"empty_message\", \"\", \"field\"},\n\t\t{\"empty_all\", \"\", \"\"},\n\t\t{\"with_special_chars\", \"Error: value > 100\", \"data.field\"},\n\t\t{\"long_message\", \"This is a very long error message that contains a lot of detail about what went wrong\", \"config\"},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, mapping := range errorTypeMappings {\n\t\tfor _, scenario := range testScenarios {\n\t\t\tt.Run(mapping.stringType+\"_\"+scenario.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t\t// Test string-based FormatError\n\t\t\t\tstringResult := FormatError(mapping.stringType, scenario.message, scenario.fieldName)\n\n\t\t\t\t// Test ErrorType-based FormatErrorWithType\n\t\t\t\tenumResult := FormatErrorWithType(mapping.enumType, scenario.message, scenario.fieldName)\n\n\t\t\t\t// They should produce identical results (for valid error types)\n\t\t\t\tif stringResult != enumResult {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"String result: %q\", stringResult)\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Enum result:   %q\", enumResult)\n\t\t\t\t\t// For case differences, this is expected - but for lowercase they should match\n\t\t\t\t\tif strings.ToLower(stringResult) != strings.ToLower(enumResult) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"String and ErrorType results should match (case-insensitive)\")\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Verify result contains error type\n\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(stringResult, mapping.stringType) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Result should contain error type %q, got: %q\", mapping.stringType, stringResult)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Verify result is not empty\n\t\t\t\tif stringResult == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Result should not be empty for error type %q\", mapping.stringType)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestFormatError_BackwardCompatibilityEdgeCases tests edge cases for backward\n// compatibility to ensure existing behavior is maintained.\nfunc TestFormatError_BackwardCompatibilityEdgeCases(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname         string\n\t\terrorType    string\n\t\tmessage      string\n\t\tfieldName    string\n\t\tshouldWork   bool\n\t\tdescription  string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"Valid error type - required\",\n\t\t\terrorType:   \"required\",\n\t\t\tmessage:     \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:   \"email\",\n\t\t\tshouldWork:  true,\n\t\t\tdescription: \"Standard required field error\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"Valid error type - error\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"Custom error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType:   \"my_custom_error\",\n\t\t\tmessage:     \"Custom validation failed\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:   \"field\",\n\t\t\tshouldWork:  true,\n\t\t\tdescription: \"Custom error types should work for backward compatibility\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"HTTP-specific error types should work\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"Error type with underscores\",\n\t\t\terrorType:   \"error_message_validation\",\n\t\t\tmessage:     \"Message validation failed\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:   \"response\",\n\t\t\tshouldWork:  true,\n\t\t\tdescription: \"Complex error types with underscores should work\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"Error type with numbers\",\n\t\t\terrorType:   \"error_404\",\n\t\t\tmessage:     \"Not found\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:   \"endpoint\",\n\t\t\tshouldWork:  true,\n\t\t\tdescription: \"Error types with numbers should work\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"Empty error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType:   \"\",\n\t\t\tmessage:     \"Generic error\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:   \"field\",\n\t\t\tshouldWork:  true,\n\t\t\tdescription: \"Empty error type should fall back to 'error'\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// This should never tt.description)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify the result is well-formed\n\t\t\tif result != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t// Should start with [error_type] or [error] for empty type\n\t\t\t\tif cases.\nfunc TestFormatError_CrossFunctionConsistency(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test that FormatErrorMessage and FormatErrorWithType work together\n\ttestCases := []struct {\n\t\terrorType  ErrorType\n\t\tmessage    string\n\t\tfieldName  string\n\t}{\n\t\t{ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\"},\n\t\t{ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid format\", \"data\"},\n\t\t{ErrTypeRange, \"Out of \"Already exists\", \"id\"},\n\t\t{ErrTypeConflict, \"Conflict detected\", \"resource\"},\n\t\t{ErrTypeUnknown, \"Unknown issue\", \"field\"},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tc := range testCases tc.fieldName)\n\t\t\tresult2 := FormatErrorMessage(tc.errorType.String(), tc.message, tc.fieldName)\n\n\t\t\t// They should produce the same result\n\t\t\tif result1 != result2 {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatErrorWithType and FormatErrorMessage should produce same result:\\n  FormatErrorWithType: %q\\n  FormatErrorMessage: %q\",\n\t\t\t\t\tresult1, result2)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Unlike\n8\tValidationErrorType which is specific to HTTP/API validation, ErrorType provides\n9\tgeneric error type classifications for any validation scenario.\n10\t\n11\tCommon ErrorType values include:\n12\t  - ErrTypeRequired: Required field is missing or empty\n13\t  - ErrTypeFormat: Value format is invalid (e.g., email, UUID pattern)\n14\t  - ErrTypeRange: Value is outside acceptable range (min/max)\n15\t  - ErrTypeLength: String length or collection size is invalid\n16\t  - ErrTypeType: Value type is incorrect (e.g., string when int expected)\n17\t  - ErrTypeValue: Value is invalid for other reasons\n18\t  - ErrTypeDuplicate: Duplicate value detected\n19\t  - ErrTypeConflict: Conflict with existing values or constraints\n20\t  - ErrTypeUnknown: Unknown error type (default/fallback)\n21\t*/\n22\t\n23\timport (\n24\t\t\"fmt\"\n25\t\t\"strings\"\n26\t)\n27\t\n28\t// =============================================================================\n29\t// ERROR TYPE ENUM\n30\t// =============================================================================\n31\t\n32\t// ErrorType is a strongly-typed enum representing common validation error categories.\n33\t// It provides type safety and prevents typos when specifying error types.\n34\t//\n35\t// ErrorType represents fundamental validation failures that can occur in any\n36\t// validation context, unlike ValidationErrorType which is specific to HTTP/API validation.\n37\t//\n38\t// Use the ErrType* constants (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat) for type-safe\n39\t// error type specification. required\"\n56\t\tErrTypeRequired ErrorType = \"required\"\n57\t\n58\t\t// ErrTypeFormat indicates a value format is invalid.\n59\t\t// Use when a value doesn't match the expected format pattern.\n60\t\t// Examples: email format, UUID pattern, date format\n61\t\t// Example: \"email format is invalid (must contain @)\"\n62\t\tErrTypeFormat ErrorType = \"format\"\n63\t\n64\t\t// Example: \"password must be at least 8 characters\"\n72\t\tErrTypeLength ErrorType = \"length\"\n73\t\n74\t\t// ErrTypeType indicates value type is incorrect.\n75\t\t// Use when a value is not of the expected type.\n76\t\t// Example: \"expected number, got string\"\n77\t\tErrTypeType ErrorType = \"type\"\n78\t\n79\t\t// ErrTypeValue indicates a value is invalid for domain-specific Unlike\nValidationErrorType which is specific to HTTP/API validation, ErrorType provides\ngeneric error type classifications for any validation scenario.\n\nCommon ErrorType values include:\n  - ErrTypeRequired: Required field is missing or empty\n  - ErrTypeFormat: Value format is invalid (e.g., email, UUID pattern)\n  - ErrTypeRange: Value is outside acceptable range (min/max)\n  - ErrTypeLength: String length or collection size is invalid\n  - ErrTypeType: Value type is incorrect (e.g., string when int expected)\n  - ErrTypeValue: Value is invalid for other reasons\n  - ErrTypeDuplicate: Duplicate value detected\n  - ErrTypeConflict: Conflict with existing values or constraints\n  - ErrTypeUnknown: Unknown error type (default/fallback)\n*/\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE ENUM\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorType is a strongly-typed enum representing common validation error categories.\n// It provides type safety and prevents typos when specifying error types.\n//\n// ErrorType represents fundamental validation failures that can occur in any\n// validation context, unlike ValidationErrorType which is specific to HTTP/API validation.\n//\n// Use the ErrType* constants (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat) for type-safe\n// error type specification. *ValidationFormatter) WithRangeInfo(info string) *ValidationFormatter {\n84\t\tvf.rangeInfo = info\n85\t\treturn vf\n86\t}\n87\t\n88\t// WithValidationDetails adds additional validation-specific details.\n89\t// Use this to provide granular information about what was checked and what failed.\n90\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithValidationDetails(details ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n91\t\tvf.validationDetails = append(vf.validationDetails, details...)\n92\t\treturn vf\n93\t}\n94\t\n95\t// WithSuggestions sets custom suggestions for fixing the validation failure.\n96\t// If not set, suggestions will be auto-generated based on validation type and values.\n97\t// Use this to override auto-generated suggestions with domain-specific guidance.\n98\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n99\t\tvf.customSuggestions = append(vf.customSuggestions, suggestions...)\n100\t\treturn vf\n101\t}\n102\t\n103\t// Format creates the final ValidationError with all configured options.\n104\t// Returns a ValidationError that implements the error interface.\n105\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) Format() ValidationError {\n106\t\tsuggestions := vf.customSuggestions\n107\t\tif len(suggestions) == 0 {\n108\t\t\t// Use auto-generated suggestions if no custom ones provided\n109\t\t\tsuggestions = generateSuggestions(vf.validationType, vf.expected, vf.actual)\n110\t\t}\n111\t\n112\t\t// Construct ValidationError directly to support custom suggestions\n113\t\treturn ValidationError{\n114\t\t\tErrorType:         vf.validationType,\n115\t\t\tExpected:          vf.expected,\n116\t\t\tActual:            vf.actual,\n117\t\t\tContext:           vf.context,\n118\t\t\tResponseSnippet:   vf.responseSnippet,\n119\t\t\tFieldName:         vf.fieldName,\n120\t\t\tPatternDetails:    vf.patternDetails,\n121\t\t\tRangeInfo:         vf.rangeInfo,\n122\t\t\tValidationDetails: vf.validationDetails,\n123\t\t\tSuggestions:       suggestions,\n124\t\t}\n125\t}\n126\t\n127\t// =============================================================================\n128\t// CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS FOR COMMON VALIDATION SCENARIOS\n129\t// =============================================================================\n130\t\n131\t// FormatStatusCodeError creates a validation error for HTTP status code mismatches.\n132\t//\n133\t// Parameters:\n134\t//   - expected: The expected status code (e.g., 200) or codes (e.g., []int{200, 201})\n135\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n136\t//   - context: Optional context about the request (e.g., \"GET /api/users\")\n137\t//\n138\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n139\t//\n140\t// Example usage:\n141\t//\n142\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users/123\")\n143\t//\t// Output:\n144\t//\t// status_code validation failed\n145\t//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n146\t//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n147\t//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n148\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n149\t//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n150\t//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n151\t//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n152\tfunc FormatStatusCodeError(expected interface{}, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\n153\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n154\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n155\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n156\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n157\t\t\tFormat()\n158\t}\n159\t\n160\t// FormatErrorMessageError creates a validation error for error message pattern mismatches.\n161\t//\n162\t// Parameters:\n163\t//   - expectedPattern: The pattern that was expected (e.g., \"invalid.*token\", \"not found\")\n164\t//   - actualMessage: The actual error message received\n165\t//   - fieldName: The field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\")\n166\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n167\t//\n168\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n169\t//\n170\t// Example usage:\n171\t//\n172\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorMessageError(\"invalid.*token\", \"access_denied\", \"error\", \"OAuth validation\")\n173\t//\t// Output:\n174\t//\t// error_message validation failed\n175\t//\t//   Expected: invalid.*token\n176\t//\t//   Actual:   access_denied\n177\t//\t//   Context:  OAuth validation\n178\t//\t//   Field:    error\n179\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n180\t//\t//     - Review the error message for specific details\n181\t//\t//     - Check API documentation for this error type\n182\t//\t//     - Verify request parameters match requirements\n183\tfunc FormatErrorMessageError(expectedPattern, actualMessage, fieldName, context string) ValidationError {\n184\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n185\t\t\tWithExpected(expectedPattern).\n186\t\t\tWithActual(actualMessage).\n187\t\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n188\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n189\t\t\tFormat()\n190\t}\n191\t\n192\t// FormatStatusCodeRangeError creates a validation error for status code range mismatches.\n193\t//\n194\t// Parameters:\n195\t//   - pattern: The range pattern (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\", \"2xx\")\n196\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n197\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n198\t//\n199\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate range information and suggestions.\n200\t//\n201\t// Example usage:\n202\t//\n203\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeRangeError(\"4xx\", 200, \"error response check\")\n204\t//\t// Output:\n205\t//\t// status_code_range validation failed\n206\t//\t//   Expected: 4xx (400-499)\n207\t//\t//   Actual:   200\n208\t//\t//   Context:  error response check\n209\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n210\t//\t//     - Review request parameters for errors\n211\t//\t//     - Check authentication credentials\n212\t//\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\n213\tfunc FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern string, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\n214\t\tmin, max, desc, err := getRangeInfo(pattern)\n215\t\n216\t\t// Build range info string\n217\t\trangeInfo := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", min, max)\n218\t\tif desc != \"\" {\n219\t\t\trangeInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d (%s)\", min, max, desc)\n220\t\t}\n221\t\n222\t\tdetails := []string{}\n223\t\tif err != nil {\n224\t\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern error: %v\", err))\n225\t\t} else {\n226\t\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is outside range %d-%d\", actual, min, max))\n227\t\t}\n228\t\n229\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code_range\").\n230\t\t\tWithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", pattern, desc)).\n231\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n232\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n233\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(details...)\n234\t\n235\t\t// Only add range info if the pattern is valid\n236\t\tif err == nil {\n237\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(rangeInfo)\n238\t\t}\n239\t\n240\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n241\t}\n242\t\n243\t// FormatContentTypeError creates a validation error for Content-Type header mismatches.\n244\t//\n245\t// Parameters:\n246\t//   - expected: The expected content type (e.g., \"application/json\")\n247\t//   - actual: The actual content type received\n248\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n249\t//\n250\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n251\t//\n252\t// Example usage:\n253\t//\n254\t//\terr := validate.FormatContentTypeError(\"application/json\", \"text/html\", \"API response\")\n255\t//\t// Output:\n256\t//\t// content_type validation failed\n257\t//\t//   Expected: application/json\n258\t//\t//   Actual:   text/html\n259\t//   Context:  API response\n260\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n261\t//\t//     - Verify Content-Type header matches request body format\n262\t//\t//     - Check if charset or boundary parameters are needed\n263\t//\t//     - Ensure the body is properly formatted for the content type\n264\tfunc FormatContentTypeError(expected, actual, context string) ValidationError {\n265\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"content_type\").\n266\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n267\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n268\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n269\t\t\tFormat()\n270\t}\n271\t\n272\t// FormatCustomValidationError creates a validation error with full customization.\n273\t//\n274\t// This function allows complete control over all validation error fields, including\n275\t// custom suggestions. Use this when the predefined convenience functions don't meet\n276\t// your specific requirements.\n277\t//\n278\t// Parameters:\n279\t//   - validationType: The category of validation\n280\t//   - expected: The expected value\n281\t//   - actual: The actual value received\n282\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions to customize the error\n283\t//\n284\t// Example usage:\n285\t//\n286\t//\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\n287\t//\t    \"custom_field\",\n288\t//\t    \"required_value\",\n289\t//\t    \"actual_value\",\n290\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"custom validation\"),\n291\t//\t    validate.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"field\": \"actual_value\"}`),\n292\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Check field value\", \"Verify configuration\"),\n293\t//\t)\n294\tfunc FormatCustomValidationError(\n295\t\tvalidationType string,\n296\t\texpected, actual interface{},\n297\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n298\t) ValidationError {\n299\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n300\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n301\t\t\topt(config)\n302\t\t}\n303\t\n304\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(validationType).\n305\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n306\t\t\tWithActual(actual)\n307\t\n308\t\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n309\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\n310\t\t}\n311\t\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n312\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\n313\t\t}\n314\t\tif config.FieldName != \"\" {\n315\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\n316\t\t}\n317\t\tif config.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n318\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n319\t\t}\n320\t\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n321\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n322\t\t}\n323\t\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n324\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n325\t\t}\n326\t\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n327\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n328\t\t}\n329\t\n330\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n331\t}\n332\t\n333\t// =============================================================================\n334\t// FORMAT OPTIONS FOR CUSTOM VALIDATION\n335\t// =============================================================================\n336\t\n337\t// FormatConfig holds configuration options for validation error formatting.\n338\ttype FormatConfig struct {\n339\t\tContext            string\n340\t\tResponseSnippet    string\n341\t\tFieldName          string\n342\t\tPatternDetails     string\n343\t\tRangeInfo          string\n344\t\tValidationDetails  []string\n345\t\tSuggestions        []string\n346\t}\n347\t\n348\t// FormatOption is a function that configures a FormatConfig.\n349\ttype FormatOption func(*FormatConfig)\n350\t\n351\t// WithContext creates a FormatOption that sets the validation context.\n352\tfunc WithContext(context string) FormatOption {\n353\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n354\t\t\tc.Context = context\n355\t\t}\n356\t}\n357\t\n358\t// WithResponseSnippet creates a FormatOption that sets the response snippet.\n359\tfunc WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) FormatOption {\n360\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n361\t\t\tc.ResponseSnippet = snippet\n362\t\t}\n363\t}\n364\t\n365\t// WithFieldName creates a FormatOption that sets the field name.\n366\tfunc WithFieldName(fieldName string) FormatOption {\n367\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n368\t\t\tc.FieldName = fieldName\n369\t\t}\n370\t}\n371\t\n372\t// WithPatternDetails creates a FormatOption that sets pattern details.\n373\tfunc WithPatternDetails(details string) FormatOption {\n374\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n375\t\t\tc.PatternDetails = details\n376\t\t}\n377\t}\n378\t\n379\t// WithRangeInfo creates a FormatOption that sets range information.\n380\tfunc WithRangeInfo(info string) FormatOption {\n381\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n382\t\t\tc.RangeInfo = info\n383\t\t}\n384\t}\n385\t\n386\t// WithValidationDetails creates a FormatOption that adds validation details.\n387\tfunc WithValidationDetails(details ...string) FormatOption {\n388\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n389\t\t\tc.ValidationDetails = append(c.ValidationDetails, details...)\n390\t\t}\n391\t}\n392\t\n393\t// WithSuggestions creates a FormatOption that sets custom suggestions.\n394\tfunc WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) FormatOption {\n395\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n396\t\t\tc.Suggestions = append(c.Suggestions, suggestions...)\n397\t\t}\n398\t}\n399\t\n400\t// =============================================================================\n401\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n402\t// =============================================================================\n403\t\n404\t// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\n405\tfunc getRangeInfo(pattern string) (min, max int, desc string, err error) {\n406\t\tmin, max, err = ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern)\n407\t\tif err != nil {\n408\t\t\treturn 0, 0, \"\", err\n409\t\t}\n410\t\n411\t\tdesc, err = GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\n412\t\tif err != nil {\n413\t\t\t// Return parsed range even if description fails\n414\t\t\tdesc = \"\"\n415\t\t}\n416\t\n417\t\treturn min, max, desc, nil\n418\t}\n419\t\n420\t// =============================================================================\n421\t// ERROR TYPE VALIDATION TRACKING\n422\t// =============================================================================\n423\t\n424\t// errorTypeTracker tracks invalid error types encountered during validation.\n425\t// This helps identify typos, deprecated error types, or configuration issues.\n426\ttype errorTypeTracker struct {\n427\t\tmu       sync.RWMutex\n428\t\tinvalidTypes map[string]int // error type -> count\n429\t}\n430\t\n431\t// global tracker instance\n432\tvar globalErrorTypeTracker = &errorTypeTracker{\n433\t\tinvalidTypes: make(map[string]int),\n434\t}\n435\t\n436\t// TrackInvalidErrorType records an invalid error type for later analysis.\n437\t// This is called automatically by FormatError when an error type doesn't\n438\t// match any known ErrorType enum value.\n439\t//\n440\t// Parameters:\n441\t//   - errorType: The invalid error type string that was encountered\n442\t//\n443\t// Returns the current count for this error type (how many times it's been seen).\n444\tfunc TrackInvalidErrorType(errorType string) int {\n445\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n446\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n447\t\n448\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]++\n449\t\treturn globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]\n450\t}\n451\t\n452\t// GetInvalidErrorTypes returns a snapshot of all invalid error types encountered\n453\t// and their occurrence counts. This is useful for debugging and identifying\n454\t// configuration issues.\n455\t//\n456\t// Returns a copy of the invalid types map to avoid concurrent modification issues.\n457\tfunc GetInvalidErrorTypes() map[string]int {\n458\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n459\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n460\t\n461\t\tresult := make(map[string]int, len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes))\n462\t\tfor k, v := range globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes {\n463\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n464\t\t}\n465\t\treturn result\n466\t}\n467\t\n468\t// ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking clears all tracked invalid error types.\n469\t// This is primarily useful for testing to ensure a clean state between tests.\n470\tfunc ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking() {\n471\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n472\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n473\t\n474\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes = make(map[string]int)\n475\t}\n476\t\n477\t// InvalidErrorTypeCount returns the total number of invalid error types tracked\n478\t// (not the total occurrences, but the number of unique invalid types).\n479\tfunc InvalidErrorTypeCount() int {\n480\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n481\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n482\t\n483\t\treturn len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes)\n484\t}\n485\t\n486\t// =============================================================================\n487\t// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n488\t// =============================================================================\n489\t\n490\t// FormatError creates a basic formatted error message string.\n491\t// This is a simplified formatting function for basic error message display.\n492\t// Unlike the ValidationFormatter which returns ValidationError structs,\n493\t// this function returns a simple formatted string for quick error display.\n494\t//\n495\t// This function supports both string-based error types (for backward compatibility)\n496\t// and validates them against the ErrorType enum for type-safe error classification.\n497\t// The ErrorType enum provides consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\n498\t//\n499\t// Error Type Validation:\n500\t//   - String error types are validated against the ErrorType enum (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"range\")\n501\t//   - If the error type is not a recognized ErrorType, it is tracked for debugging purposes\n502\t//   - Invalid error types do NOT cause errors - the original string is still used for backward compatibility\n503\t//   - To check what invalid error types have been encountered, use GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n504\t//   - To reset tracking between tests, use ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n505\t//\n506\t// Parameters:\n507\t//   - errorType: The type/category of error (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"status_code\")\n508\t//   - message: The error message content\n509\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n510\t//\n511\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n512\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n513\t//\n514\t// Example usage:\n515\t//\n516\t//\t// Valid error types (recognized by ErrorType enum)\n517\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"required\", \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n518\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n519\t//\n520\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n521\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n522\t//\n523\t//\t// Custom/unknown error types (still work, but are tracked)\n524\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"custom_validation\", \"Custom check failed\", \"field\")\n525\t//\t// Returns: \"[custom_validation] field: Custom check failed\"\n526\t//\t// \"custom_validation\" is now tracked as an invalid error type\n527\t//\n528\t//\t// Check what invalid types have been encountered\n529\t//\tinvalidTypes := validate.GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n530\t//\t// Returns: map[string]int{\"custom_validation\": 1, ...}\n531\t//\n532\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"\", \"Something went wrong\", \"\")\n533\t//\t// Returns: \"[error] Something went wrong\"\n534\t//\n535\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"validation\", \"\", \"email\")\n536\t//\t// Returns: \"[validation] email: email validation failed\"\n537\tfunc FormatError(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\n538\t\t// Extract field name from variadic args first\n539\t\tfieldNameStr := \"\"\n540\t\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\n541\t\t\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\n542\t\t}\n543\t\n544\t\t// Check if errorType is whitespace-only (not truly empty)\n545\t\t// Whitespace-only error types should be tracked as invalid\n546\t\t// because they are likely user errors, not intentional defaults\n547\t\toriginalHasContent := errorType != \"\"\n548\t\ttrimmedType := strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n549\t\tisWhitespaceOnly := originalHasContent && trimmedType == \"\"\n550\t\n551\t\t// Track whitespace-only error types as invalid\n552\t\tif isWhitespaceOnly {\n553\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n554\t\t}\n555\t\n556\t\t// Trim whitespace from errorType before validation\n557\t\terrorType = trimmedType\n558\t\n559\t\t// Validate string errorType against ErrorType enum\n560\t\t// This ensures that string-based error types are checked for validity\n561\t\t// while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value\n562\t\tvalidatedType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n563\t\n564\t\t// Track invalid error types for debugging\n565\t\t// If ErrorTypeFromString returns ErrTypeUnknown but the trimmed errorType wasn't \"unknown\" (case-insensitive),\n566\t\t// it means we encountered an unrecognized error type string\n567\t\tif validatedType == ErrTypeUnknown && errorType != \"\" && !strings.EqualFold(errorType, \"unknown\") {\n568\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n569\t\t}\n570\t\n571\t\t// Trim whitespace from message and fieldName before checking\n572\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n573\t\tfieldNameStr = strings.TrimSpace(fieldNameStr)\n574\t\n575\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n576\t\tif message == \"\" {\n577\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided (after trimming)\n578\t\t\tif fieldNameStr != \"\" {\n579\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldNameStr)\n580\t\t\t} else {\n581\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n582\t\t\t}\n583\t\t}\n584\t\n585\t\t// Handle empty errorType (after trimming) - use fallback\n586\t\tif errorType == \"\" {\n587\t\t\terrorType = \"error\"\n588\t\t}\n589\t\n590\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage with the processed error type string\n591\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\n592\t}\n593\t\n594\t// FormatErrorWithType creates a basic formatted error message string using ErrorType enum.\n595\t// This function provides type-safe error classification using the ErrorType enum\n596\t// instead of string-based error types.\n597\t//\n598\t// This is the preferred method for error formatting when you have an ErrorType enum\n599\t// value, as it ensures consistent error classification and prevents typos.\n600\t//\n601\t// Parameters:\n602\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n603\t//   - message: The error message content\n604\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n605\t//\n606\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n607\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n608\t//\n609\t// Example usage:\n610\t//\n611\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n612\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n613\t//\n614\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n615\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n616\t//\n617\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRange, \"Value out of range\", \"age\")\n618\t//\t// Returns: \"[range] age: Value out of range\"\n619\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n620\t\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\n621\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n622\t\n623\t\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\n624\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n625\t\n626\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n627\t\tif message == \"\" {\n628\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n629\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n630\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n631\t\t\t} else {\n632\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n633\t\t\t}\n634\t\t}\n635\t\n636\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\n637\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n638\t}\n639\t\n640\t// =============================================================================\n641\t// FIELD REFERENCE QUOTE STYLES\n642\t// =============================================================================\n643\t\n644\t// QuoteStyle defines the quoting style for field names in field references.\n645\ttype QuoteStyle string\n646\t\n647\tconst (\n648\t\t// NoQuote applies no quotes to field names\n649\t\tNoQuote QuoteStyle = \"\"\n650\t\t// SingleQuote applies single quotes around field names\n651\t\tSingleQuote QuoteStyle = \"'\"\n652\t\t// DoubleQuote applies double quotes around field names\n653\t\tDoubleQuote QuoteStyle = \"\\\"\"\n654\t\t// Backtick applies backtick quotes around field names\n655\t\tBacktick QuoteStyle = \"`\"\n656\t)\n657\t\n658\t// FieldRefConfig holds configuration options for field reference formatting.\n659\ttype FieldRefConfig struct {\n660\t\tquoteStyle QuoteStyle\n661\t\tprefix     string\n662\t}\n663\t\n664\t// FieldRefOption is a function that configures a FieldRefConfig.\n665\ttype FieldRefOption func(*FieldRefConfig)\n666\t\n667\t// WithQuoteStyle creates a FieldRefOption that sets the quote style for field names.\n668\t// The quote style is applied to each field name component (not to array indices or prefixes).\n669\t//\n670\t// Example usage:\n671\t//\n672\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n673\t//\t// Returns: WithQuoteStyle(style QuoteStyle) FieldRefOption {\n678\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n679\t\t\tc.quoteStyle = style\n680\t\t}\n681\t}\n682\t\n683\t// WithPrefix creates a FieldRefOption that sets the prefix for field references.\n684\t// The prefix is added before the field path (e.g., \"response\", \"request\", \"data\").\n685\t// The prefix is not affected by quote styles and is added as-is.\n686\t//\n687\t// If both the prefix parameter and WithPrefix option are provided, the option takes precedence.\n688\t//\n689\t// Example usage:\n690\t//\n691\t//\tref := =============================================================================\n706\t// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\n707\t// =============================================================================\n708\t\n709\t// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\n710\t// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\n711\t// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\n712\t//\n713\t// Parameters:\n714\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n715\t//\n716\t// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\n717\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n718\t//\n719\t// Example usage:\n720\t//\n721\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"user.email\")\n722\t//\t// Returns: \"user.email\"\n723\t//\n724\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"users.0.email\")\n725\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n726\t//\n727\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"data.items[5]\")\n728\t//\t// Returns: \"data.items[5]\"\n729\t//\n730\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"\")\n731\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n732\tfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\n733\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n734\t\n735\t\t// Handle empty path\n736\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n737\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n738\t\t}\n739\t\n740\t\t// Parse and normalize the field path\n741\t\treturn normalizeFieldPath(fieldPath)\n742\t}\n743\t\n744\t// normalizeFieldPath processes a field path and normalizes array notation.\n745\t// It converts \"users.0.email\" to \"users[0].email\" format for consistency.\n746\tfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\n747\t\tif path == \"\" {\n748\t\t\treturn \"\"\n749\t\t}\n750\t\n751\t\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n752\t\tvar result []string\n753\t\n754\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n755\t\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n756\t\t\tif part == \"\" {\n757\t\t\t\tcontinue\n758\t\t\t}\n759\t\n760\t\t\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\n761\t\t\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\n762\t\t\t\t// Convert \".0\" to \"[0]\" format\n763\t\t\t\tif len(result) > 0 {\n764\t\t\t\t\t// Append to previous part as array index\n765\t\t\t\t\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\n766\t\t\t\t\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s[%s]\", result[lastIdx], part)\n767\t\t\t\t} else {\n768\t\t\t\t\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\n769\t\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n770\t\t\t\t}\n771\t\t\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\n772\t\t\t\t// Already has array notation like \"items[5]\" - keep as is\n773\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n774\t\t\t} else {\n775\t\t\t\t// Regular field name\n776\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n777\t\t\t}\n778\t\t}\n779\t\n780\t\tif len(result) == 0 {\n781\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n782\t\t}\n783\t\n784\t\treturn strings.Join(result, \".\")\n785\t}\n786\t\n787\t// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\n788\tfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\n789\t\tif s == \"\" {\n790\t\t\treturn false\n791\t\t}\n792\t\n793\t\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\n794\t\tif s[0] == '-' {\n795\t\t\tif len(s) == 1 {\n796\t\t\t\treturn false\n797\t\t\t}\n798\t\t\ts = s[1:]\n799\t\t}\n800\t\n801\t\t// Check if all characters are digits\n802\t\tfor _, r := range s {\n803\t\t\tif r < '0' || r > '9' {\n804\t\t\t\treturn false\n805\t\t\t}\n806\t\t}\n807\t\n808\t\treturn true\n809\t}\n810\t\n811\t// containsArrayNotation checks if a string already contains array notation like \"[5]\".\n812\tfunc containsArrayNotation(s string) bool {\n813\t\treturn strings.Contains(s, \"[\")\n814\t}\n815\t\n816\t// FormatFieldReference creates a standardized field reference string with an It supports optional quote styles\n819\t// for field names through variadic options.\n820\t//\n821\t// Parameters:\n822\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", formatting (e.g., WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n825\t//\n826\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n827\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\"\n854\t//\n855\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"request\")\n856\t//\t// Returns: \"request\"\n857\tfunc FormatFieldReference(fieldPath string, prefix string, options ...FieldRefOption) \"\"}\n863\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n864\t\t\topt(config)\n865\t\t}\n866\t\n867\t\t// If prefix was set via option, use it (overrides the direct parameter)\n868\t\tif config.prefix != \"\" {\n869\t\t\tprefix = config.prefix\n870\t\t}\n871\t\n872\t\t// Handle empty field path\n873\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n874\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n875\t\t\t\t// Normalize prefix even when field is QuoteStyle) string {\n920\t\tif path == \"(unknown field)\" || path == \"\" {\n921\t\t\treturn path\n922\t\t}\n923\t\n924\t\tvar result strings.Builder\n925\t\tvar fieldName r == '[':\n943\t\t\t\t// Start of array index, flush field name first\n944\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n945\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n946\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n947\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n948\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n949\t\t\t\t}\n950\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('[')\n951\t\t\t\tinBrackets FormatValidationErrorWithDetails functionality with a more ergonomic interface.\ntype ValidationFormatter struct {\n\tvalidationType string\n\texpected       interface{}\n\tactual \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tformatter := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n//\t    WithExpected(200).\n//\t    WithActual(404).\n//\t
